commit.message:
Reduce placement density and hold slack margins

PL_TARGET_DENSITY_PCT 75→55: gives more room for hold buffer insertion
PL_RESIZER_HOLD_SLACK_MARGIN 0.1→0.05: fewer hold violations to fix
GRT_RESIZER_HOLD_SLACK_MARGIN 0.05→0.025: same

Previous builds failed with DPL-0036 (detailed placement failed)
because hold repair inserted thousands of buffers with no room.

refactor: split AWS/MCP deps into optional extra for Raspberry Pi deployment

Move awsiotsdk, boto3, and fastmcp into [project.optional-dependencies] aws-mcp
so the local agent can be installed without the AWS/MCP stack.

- Raspberry Pi: `uv sync --no-dev`
- Dev machine: `uv sync --extra aws-mcp`

Update CLAUDE.md and README.md to document the split and install commands.

commit.message:
feat(lsp): add semantic token classifier module

Implements computeSemanticTokens() — a second-pass token classifier
that maps lexer tokens to LSP semantic types via a lightweight state
machine. Exports TOKEN_LEGEND (tokenTypes + tokenModifiers arrays).
Adds 30 tests covering all token types, modifiers, contexts, delta
encoding, and error resilience.

commit.message:
feat(state-maps): add Male LFPR and Total LFPR indicators from PLFS

Adds two new PLFS annual choropleth indicators:
- LFPR (Total): all-persons LFPR, combined rural+urban, age 15+, PS+SS (Blues)
- Male LFPR: male-only LFPR, combined rural+urban, age 15+, PS+SS (Oranges)

Data sourced from MoSPI PLFS API (indicator_code=1, frequency_code=1,
gender_code=1/3, sector_code=3) covering all 7 annual rounds 2017-18
to 2023-24 across 36 states/UTs (Ladakh and D&NH&DD have partial nulls).

Changes:
- plfs-state.json: added lfpr_male[] and lfpr_person[] arrays per state
- types.ts: extended PLFSStateSeries with lfpr_male and lfpr_person fields
- useMospiState.ts: extended plfsStateSlice indicator union type
- state-maps.tsx: added lfpr_person and lfpr_male INDICATOR entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(wq-840): guard against non-array threads_contributed in conversation-balance

threads_contributed in engagement-trace.json can be a number (legacy format)
instead of an array. The || [] fallback doesn't catch truthy non-arrays.
Replaced all 4 occurrences with Array.isArray guards. Also hardened
agents_interacted at the same call sites.
